mechanism: Azure roles assigned to Entra ID groups, not individuals. Platform owns groups and role bindings; membership managed in Entra ID.
why: Individual assignments couple RBAC to user lifecycle — a deleted user GUID fails Bicep deployment, and every onboard/offboard requires redeployment. Groups separate composition from permission grants — membership changes take effect without touching any deployment.

related.note: Custom roles give groups semantic meaning — role name describes function, not resource.
Landing zone identity RBAC references groups — membership controls who can assume permissions.
Group-based access only works because local auth is denied — access keys would bypass groups.

violations: Individual user role assignment at management group level.
